muxt generate --use-receiver-type=Server
muxt check

exec go test -race -count=1

-- template.gohtml --
{{- define "GET /events sse(Events(sendClock, send, sendMetrics))" -}}main:{{- .Result -}}{{- end -}}
{{- define "Clock" -}}clock:{{- .Result -}}{{- end -}}
{{- define "Metrics" -}}metrics:{{- .Result -}}{{- end -}}
-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.24
-- server.go --
package server

import (
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type Server struct{}

// Events receives three send callbacks in non-canonical order (the base "send"
// is not first) to prove ordering is irrelevant. The "send" callback renders the
// route template; sendClock renders the "Clock" template; sendMetrics renders
// the "Metrics" template.
func (Server) Events(sendClock, send, sendMetrics func(data string) error) {
	_ = send("main")
	_ = sendClock("tick")
	_ = sendMetrics("cpu")
}
-- server_test.go --
package server

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

func TestEvents(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, Server{})

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/events", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	body := rec.Body.String()
	for _, want := range []string{"data: main:main\n\n", "data: clock:tick\n\n", "data: metrics:cpu\n\n"} {
		if !strings.Contains(body, want) {
			t.Fatalf("body = %q, want it to contain %q", body, want)
		}
	}
}
